Event: Santana gives Brittany an anguished declaration of love
Explanation: She gains emotional honesty but is rejected and hurt while giving Brittany clarity and respect for her existing relationship.

Event: Brittany rejects Santana due to already dating Artie
Explanation: Brittany preserves her current relationship and personal boundaries while avoiding cheating, though it hurts Santana.

Event: Santana chooses to go to prom with Karofsky and pretend to be straight
Explanation: She gains short-term social protection by reinforcing a lie, while sustaining a homophobic/closeted environment.

Event: Finn publicly asks Santana why she doesn’t come out of the closet
Explanation: He vents his hurt and briefly “wins” the exchange but triggers harsh fallout for Santana and wider homophobic backlash.

Event: Karofsky threatens Kurt and Blaine
Explanation: He asserts dominance and preserves his image at the cost of directly menacing others.

Event: Santana confronts Karofsky and threatens to hurt him if he attacks Kurt and Blaine
Explanation: She raises her own status as tough while effectively protecting Kurt and Blaine from further bullying.

Event: Santana and Karofsky attend prom together as each other’s beards
Explanation: They gain reputational cover and popularity while sustaining deception and heteronormative pressure on others.

Event: Santana steals a wheelbarrow of breadsticks from the restaurant
Explanation: She gains a lot of free food while financially harming the restaurant.

Event: Santana gets the Breadstix manager fired for trying to stop her theft
Explanation: She removes an obstacle and exerts power but seriously harms the manager’s livelihood.

Event: Santana breaks up with Puck after seeing his bad credit score
Explanation: She protects her own financial ambitions while callously discarding Puck.

Event: Santana plans to marry a football player for financial stability
Explanation: She aims for personal security while intending an exploitative marriage.

Event: Santana gets a boob job to gain more attention from guys
Explanation: She undergoes risky surgery that quickly backfires and damages her reputation, while momentarily pleasing male onlookers.

Event: Santana fights Lauren over Puck
Explanation: She tries to secure Puck for herself but is physically outmatched and fails, while creating conflict.

Event: Santana engages in catfights with Mercedes and Quinn over boys/Santana's spot
Explanation: She tries to defend status and relationships using aggression, harming others while gaining little lasting benefit.

Event: Santana has to be restrained from attacking Rachel
Explanation: She attempts physical aggression, risking punishment, for a fleeting sense of dominance.

Event: Santana occasionally acts as Brittany’s minder
Explanation: She expends some effort to guide Brittany while giving Brittany safety and structure.

Event: Santana quits the Cheerios
Explanation: She sacrifices status to align with people she cares about, also weakening Sue’s power over the group.

Event: Sue revokes Santana’s tanning privileges
Explanation: Sue asserts control and perhaps enforces discipline while inflicting disproportionate emotional distress.

Event: Santana cries and flees the room when Sue revokes tanning
Explanation: She gets no benefit and publicly humiliates herself while not affecting others beyond mild discomfort.

Event: Santana gets Breadstix manager fired over stopping her theft (second claim)
Explanation: Again emphasizes she wields institutional power to punish resistance for personal convenience.

Event: Santana originally joins glee club as a mole for Sue
Explanation: She gains favor with Sue while undermining trust and cohesion in the club.

Event: Santana uses her sass to help Kurt and Blaine stand up to Karofsky
Explanation: She strengthens their confidence and protection while also boosting her own sense of purpose and alliances.

Event: Santana’s antagonism toward the glee club members
Explanation: Her habitual meanness bolsters her tough persona but erodes relationships and team morale.

Event: Will kicks Santana out of glee club after she sabotages recruitment
Explanation: He loses a strong performer but protects the group’s integrity and signals consequences for sabotage.

Event: Santana constantly harasses Finn and Rory in “Mash-Off”
Explanation: She asserts dominance and vents anger but provokes Finn into an outing that badly hurts her.

Event: Finn publicly outs Santana in the hallway
Explanation: He feels vindicated and “honest” in the moment but causes Santana severe personal and social damage.

Event: Finn’s outing of Santana contributes to her being outed statewide
Explanation: He gains nothing further but amplifies the harm done to her via Sue’s campaign fallout.

Event: Santana calls Quinn a slut while being promiscuous herself
Explanation: She briefly elevates herself socially by shaming Quinn while deepening mutual hostility.

Event: Santana spurns Brittany in “Duets”
Explanation: She protects herself from vulnerability but damages Brittany and their relationship.

Event: Santana dumps Brittany in “The Break-Up” under an “energy exchange” pretense
Explanation: She escapes a relationship she can’t currently maintain while inflicting deep emotional harm on Brittany.

Event: Santana uses “keeping it real” as an excuse to throw snark at people
Explanation: She maintains her persona and deflects her own issues by belittling others.

Event: Santana gets drunk and becomes hysterical
Explanation: She emotionally unravels and harms her own dignity without meaningful effect on others.

Event: Santana repeatedly pursues relationships with boys to suppress feelings for Brittany
Explanation: She damages her own emotional clarity and misleads partners while normalizing them socially.

Event: Santana realizes in “Sexy” that her cruelty stems from turmoil over Brittany
Explanation: The insight hurts but enables growth and more honest future choices, indirectly benefiting others she mistreats.

Event: Santana convinces Brittany that sex with her isn’t cheating on Artie because “the plumbing’s different”
Explanation: She secures an affair for herself while morally and emotionally undermining Brittany and Artie’s relationship.

Event: Santana borrows mono germs then kisses Finn to infect him and Quinn
Explanation: She cleverly exposes their cheating and gains leverage by intentionally making them sick.

Event: Santana convinces Sam to dump Quinn for her
Explanation: She successfully secures Sam at the cost of their relationship and their emotional well-being.

Event: Santana convinces Karofsky to be her beard and start an anti-bullying patrol
Explanation: She and Karofsky gain popularity, do real good with anti-bullying, but the move is rooted in deception and self-protection.

Event: Karofsky convinces Kurt to come back to school as part of the patrol plan
Explanation: He boosts his rep and partially redeems himself by creating conditions for Kurt’s safer return.

Event: Santana pursues Prom Queen to “royally” obligate Brittany to date her
Explanation: She seeks status partly to coerce Brittany emotionally instead of relying on mutual consent.

Event: Santana sets up a sting to expose Brody as a gigolo after being kicked out
Explanation: She vindicates her suspicions and helps reveal the truth while orchestrating a public, violent humiliation.

Event: Finn uses Santana’s sting to beat Brody up
Explanation: He gets cathartic revenge and moral satisfaction while physically harming Brody.

Event: Santana stands up to Sebastian on behalf of Kurt and Blaine
Explanation: She risks conflict to protect friends, gaining moral authority and stronger bonds.

Event: Santana gives Sue a “Reason You Suck” speech in “The Quarterback”
Explanation: She gains catharsis and honesty while forcing Sue to confront the harm she’s done to the students.

Event: Santana delivers a vicious “Reason You Suck” speech to Finn in “Mash-Off”
Explanation: She momentarily wins the argument but provokes the outing that deeply damages her.

Event: Santana confronts Kurt in season 6 with her most crushing speech
Explanation: She vents and asserts herself, but humiliates Kurt and strains relationships with little benefit.

Event: Santana helps Rachel by becoming her understudy for Funny Girl and challenging her
Explanation: She gains career opportunity while pushing Rachel to grow and maintain standards.

Event: Santana calls herself “the mistress” while secretly dating Brittany
Explanation: She enjoys an illicit role and emotional connection while participating in deceit that hurts Artie.

Event: Sue sends Santana, Quinn, and Brittany into glee as moles
Explanation: Sue gains intelligence and leverage over the club while undermining its cohesion.

Event: Santana uses a cheerleader to make Brittany jealous after their breakup
Explanation: She boosts her own ego and tries to regain control by needlessly hurting Brittany.

Event: Santana sleeps with Quinn twice after dumping Brittany
Explanation: She gains pleasure and intimacy but complicates and exploits Quinn’s unresolved feelings.

Event: Santana quits Cheerios and starts dressing in normal clothes
Explanation: She loses uniform-based status but gains autonomy and more authentic self-expression.

Event: Santana repeatedly delivers brutal “Reason You Suck” speeches to various people
Explanation: She maintains power and emotional distance by verbally demolishing others, eroding relationships overall.

Event: Santana helps persuade Kurt to stand up to Karofsky and Sebastian along with others
Explanation: She strengthens Kurt’s resolve and safety while also cementing her own role as ally.

Event: Santana often acts as Brittany’s morality pet target, softening around her
Explanation: Brittany’s influence helps Santana grow and be kinder, which benefits them both.
